'''Shiffrin''' was an [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Tammuz sector/Legends|Tammuz sector]]. [[Constable/Legends|Constable]] [[Kallin Bremen]] maintained law and order in a small [[Unidentified Shiffrin village|village]] there. At one point, a Shiffrin [[Farmer/Legends|farmer]] discovered what turned out to be large deposits of the valuable [[Ore/Legends|ore]] [[hfredium]], and the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] subsequently established a [[Garrison/Legends|garrison]] there and began confiscating the properties of local landowners. Bremen was eventually [[Death/Legends|executed]] after planning a peaceful demonstration against the confiscations, and his [[Gender/Legends|son]], [[Jak Bremen]], went on to leave the planet.

==Description==
Shiffrin was a terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="TTTS" /> located in the [[Shiffrin system/Legends|Shiffrin system]], a part of the [[Tammuz sector/Legends|Tammuz sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> in [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="EAOC" /> The planet had large deposits of [[hfredium]], a semi-precious [[Ore/Legends|ore]] used in [[Weapon/Legends|weapons]] manufacturing.<ref name="TTTS" />

==History==
[[File:Jak_Bremen.jpg|thumb|right|180px|Jak Bremen grew up on Shiffrin.]]
At one point during the [[Imperial Era/Legends|reign]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], a [[Farmer/Legends|farmer]] on Shiffrin discovered a small amount of hfredium. When he reported the find to the authorities—in the hopes of obtaining a reward in return—an Imperial [[Garrison/Legends|garrison]] was instead established on the planet and the farmer's land was confiscated. As Shiffrin's hfredium deposits, initially believed to be relatively small, were discovered to actually be much larger than was predicted, an increasing number of other landowners had their properties seized, as well, with the countryside being slowly carved up in order to meet the needs of the [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Military]].<ref name="TTTS" />

Arguing that the standard Imperial procedure for the confiscation of private property was thus being violated, [[Kallin Bremen]], the chief [[Constable/Legends|constable]] of a Shiffrin [[Unidentified Shiffrin village|village]], protested against the Empire's actions, only to see his complaints ignored. Bremen subsequently organized the planet's disgruntled landowners and planned and led a peaceful demonstration to make their concerns known to the [[Sector/Legends|sector's]] Imperial [[Moff/Legends|Moff]]. The [[Commander/Legends|commander]] of the Shiffrin garrison, however, claiming the group were agitators of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], immediately arrested the protesters, and the Moff ordered them all [[Death/Legends|executed]]. The surviving Shiffrin locals were shocked by the atrocity but also frightened into compliance by fear of further mass executions. Bremen's young [[Gender/Legends|son]], [[Jak Bremen|Jak]], escaped the planet aboard a [[Mining/Legends|mining]] [[Transport/Legends|transport]], going on to wander the Outer Rim Territories.<ref name="TTTS" />

==Locations==
Shiffrin was the site of a small village where the constable Kallin Bremen maintained law and order and [[Parent/Legends|raised]] his son, the [[Human/Legends|Human]] Jak. Eventually, an Imperial garrison was also established on the planet.<ref name="TTTS" />

==Behind the scenes==
Shiffrin was first mentioned in the [[1994]] ''[[The Last Command Sourcebook]]'', written by [[Eric S. Trautmann|Eric Trautmann]] for [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="TLC">''[[The Last Command Sourcebook]]''</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Shiffrin [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the planet itself, in grid square T-15.<ref name="TEA" />
